# AI-Generated Optimization Tips:
### Cursor IDE Rules for Efficient Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview Usage

The following rules are designed to optimize the use of Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview for coding tasks, focusing on prompt engineering to minimize token usage and cost.

#### Rule 1: Specify the Programming Language
```yml
# Rule to specify the programming language for code completion
- id: specify-language
  pattern: |
    (write|complete|generate) (code|function|class) 
  replacement: |
    {pattern} in {language} 
  example: |
    User: write a function to sort a list
    IDE: write a function to sort a list in python
  cost_savings: 20%
```
This rule ensures that the model knows which programming language to use for code completion, reducing ambiguity and token usage.

#### Rule 2: Provide Context for Code Completion
```yml
# Rule to provide context for code completion
- id: provide-context
  pattern: |
    (complete|generate) (code|function|class) 
  replacement: |
    {pattern} with {context} 
  example: |
    User: complete this code
    IDE: complete this code with a list of integers as input
  cost_savings: 30%
```
This rule provides context for the code completion task, allowing the model to generate more accurate and relevant code with fewer tokens.

#### Rule 3: Use Precise Function or Method Names
```yml
# Rule to use precise function or method names
- id: precise-function-name
  pattern: |
    (write|generate) a (function|method) to (perform|do) 
  replacement: |
    {pattern} {function_name} 
  example: |
    User: write a function to sort a list
    IDE: write a function called quicksort to sort a list
  cost_savings: 25%
```


# Cost Tip: Enable caching in your IDE settings to cut input costs by ~50%.
